novel_features.name: Live event watch with dedup + pluggable notifier
novel_features.description: Long-running poll of a USGS summary feed with deduplication against the local store. Invokes an optional shell hook per new event for custom alerting.
novel_features.rationale: Polls the GeoJSON summary feed, dedups against the local earthquakes table by event ID, and invokes a user-provided shell hook per new event. No competing tool offers polling; competing MCPs are request/response only.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you want a long-running monitor over USGS earthquakes without writing a polling loop. Pluggable shell hook means it composes with notify-send, Slack curl, paging systems, anything.

novel_features.name: Aftershock sequence query
novel_features.description: Show events within R km and T days after a mainshock, ordered by time. Local SQLite haversine query with FDSN fallback when uncached.
novel_features.rationale: Requires haversine bounded join on (time, latitude, longitude) across the local earthquakes table. Competing MCP exposes only single-event detail; no CLI composes the sequence.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this any time you have a mainshock event ID and need to see what came after. The single-call alternative is dozens of /query invocations stitched in jq.

novel_features.name: Spatial-temporal swarm detection
novel_features.description: Detect time-space clusters in the local earthquake store. Grid-bucket clustering finds foreshock/aftershock sequences, volcanic swarms, and induced seismicity hotspots.
novel_features.rationale: Local SQLite grouping by 0.1° lat/lon cells × T-hour windows, filtered where event count ≥ N, with contiguous cells merged. No competing tool exposes clustering.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when monitoring a volcano, fault zone, or fracking region for unusual clustering. The output names each cluster with center, count, peak magnitude, and time range.

novel_features.name: Region or period comparison
novel_features.description: Side-by-side comparison of two regions or two time periods. Returns parallel columns with counts, max magnitude, and total seismic energy.
novel_features.rationale: Two parallel SELECT COUNT, MAX(mag), SUM(energy) aggregations on the local earthquakes table with delta computed. FDSN /count fallback when local data doesn't cover the windows. Research-grade comparison no competitor exposes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when answering 'is region A more active than region B?' or 'is this region quieter than it used to be?'. Outputs delta percentages and energy ratios.

novel_features.name: Newsroom event briefing
novel_features.description: Agent-ready briefing for a single earthquake: magnitude, place, PAGER alert, DYFI felt count, ShakeMap MMI, tsunami status, and product inventory.
novel_features.rationale: Composes FDSN /query GeoJSON properties and products manifest into a structured Markdown/text/JSON block. Competing MCP returns raw JSON; this is agent-native by construction.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this in newsroom or Slack contexts where you need a one-event summary an editor or downstream agent can drop straight into copy. Includes the USGS event-page URL.

novel_features.name: Editorial-rank top events
novel_features.description: Rank recent events by composite editorial score: significance × alert weight × felt count × tsunami flag. Default window 24h, limit 10.
novel_features.rationale: Local earthquakes table ORDER BY (sig * alert_weight(alert) * (1+ln(1+felt)) * (1+2*tsunami)) DESC over a windowed slice. FDSN exposes sig but no composite ranking endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you need 'the events that matter right now' rather than 'all events by magnitude'. The composite score promotes felt + PAGER + tsunami over raw magnitude.

novel_features.name: Stateful change / revision diff
novel_features.description: Diff since the last sync: what events appeared, what events had magnitudes/depths/alerts revised, what events were retracted. Tracks USGS solution revisions over time. Returns 'no revisions recorded yet' until at least two sync runs have completed.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a revisions table populated by sync comparing pre/post values per event on (mag, depth, alert, status, updated). Genuinely novel — no existing tool tracks USGS solution revisions.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a previously-automatic event might have just been reviewed by an analyst (revising magnitude or alert), or to answer 'what's new since I last looked?'

novel_features.name: USGS event ID decoder
novel_features.description: Parse a USGS event ID into its source network code, sequence, and operator name. Joins the cached contributors dictionary for the network display name.
novel_features.rationale: String parse of <network><sequence> joined against the cached contributors dictionary (populated by sync from FDSN /contributors). No tool decodes USGS event IDs today; common confusion point for new users.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you see an opaque USGS event ID (e.g. nc73947885, ak0202xyz) and need to know which network reported it before reading the data.

narrative.headline: Every USGS earthquake feed and event query in one terminal, with offline SQLite cache, agent-native output, and a live watch mode.
narrative.value_prop: Wraps the full USGS FDSN Event Service and all 20 GeoJSON summary feeds in a single binary. Adds a 30-day rolling local SQLite cache so search, sql, aftershocks, swarm-detect, top, and changes run instantly without the network. Built for seismologists, journalists, emergency managers, and agents that need to reason about earthquake activity programmatically.
narrative.auth_narrative: No authentication required. USGS earthquake services are fully public; doctor verifies reachability of both the FDSN Event base URL and the summary feeds.

quickstart.comment: List M4.5+ earthquakes in the last 24h. Default for newsroom triage.
Pull the pre-built 'past week, significant' summary feed once.
Populate the local SQLite cache (30 days, M2.5+) so search/sql/aftershocks/top/changes work offline.
Rank recent events by composite editorial score (sig × alert × felt × tsunami).
Get a one-event briefing with PAGER, DYFI, tsunami, ShakeMap MMI, and product inventory.
Investigate a mainshock's aftershock sequence from the local store.

troubleshoots.symptom: Search or sql returns empty rows on a fresh install
troubleshoots.fix: Run `usgs-earthquakes-pp-cli sync` first — the local SQLite store is empty until sync populates it.
Lower --limit below 20000 or paginate with --offset; the FDSN cap is a hard ceiling.

troubleshoots.symptom: Repeated polling slows down or returns stale data
troubleshoots.fix: Use `feeds get <feed>` (e.g. `feeds get all_hour`) for high-cadence monitoring — USGS recommends summary feeds over /query for repeated polls.

troubleshoots.symptom: watch keeps exec'ing the notify hook during printing-press verify
troubleshoots.fix: Already handled: watch checks PRINTING_PRESS_VERIFY=1 and skips the hook in mock-mode subprocesses.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI any time an agent needs to answer questions about USGS earthquakes — recent activity, historical search, single-event detail, aftershock sequences, regional comparisons, or live monitoring. It is the right choice when speed matters (offline SQLite cache), when output must be agent-friendly (--json, --select, --compact, --csv on every command), or when the workflow spans multiple events that the raw FDSN API would require stitching together. Not the right choice for non-USGS seismic networks (IRIS event service, EMSC) or for waveform/station data — those need an FDSN station-service client like obspy.

recipes.title: Did anything newsworthy happen in the last hour?
recipes.explanation: Ranks the past hour's events by composite editorial score (sig × alert × felt × tsunami) and narrows the agent context to just the editorially-relevant fields via --select.

recipes.title: Track a swarm at Mount Hood for the past 7 days
recipes.explanation: Spatial-temporal clustering around the Mount Hood coordinate; returns named clusters with center, count, peak magnitude, and time range.

recipes.title: Aftershock report for a recent mainshock
recipes.explanation: Local SQLite haversine query bounded by the mainshock's time and location; falls back to FDSN /query when the event isn't cached.

recipes.title: Daily newsroom briefing for the top event
recipes.explanation: Returns just the top-ranked event's ID; pipe to `xargs -I {} usgs-earthquakes-pp-cli brief {} --format markdown` to get a newsroom-ready briefing.

recipes.title: Find events that have a ShakeMap product near San Francisco
recipes.explanation: Uses the FDSN --product-type filter (not exposed by the competing MCP) plus the --latitude/--longitude circle filter to find events that have a ShakeMap polygon since the start of the year.
